ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'milk tea' class (FOODON_03301103); no specific entry exists for Ribena-flavoured green milk tea. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers tea-based beverages broadly; no specific code exists for green milk tea with blackcurrant syrup. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept exists for this specific commercial milk tea beverage.

serving.natural_language_portion: One large (L-size) cup of approximately 500 ml, as served at KOI Thé Singapore outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Milk tea, prepared with whole milk and sugar — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; supplemented with Ribena blackcurrant syrup composition from USDA Branded Foods and standard green tea nutrient data
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for KOI L-Ribena Green Milk Tea exists in any approved primary database. Values are estimated by component analysis: green tea base (~60% v/v), whole fresh milk (~30% v/v), Ribena blackcurrant syrup (~10% v/v), and sugar syrup. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries used for green tea (FDC ID 171917), whole milk (FDC ID 171265), and Ribena-type blackcurrant cordial (USDA Branded Foods reference). Per-100g values represent the blended ready-to-drink beverage before ice dilution.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — milk tea and sweetened tea beverages; blackcurrant juice GI reference
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for KOI L-Ribena Green Milk Tea. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on analogous sweetened milk tea beverages from the University of Sydney GI Database (sweetened milk tea GI range 47–65) and blackcurrant juice GI of approximately 40–50 (Atkinson et al., 2008, AJCN). The blended beverage contains significant added sugar from Ribena syrup and sugar syrup, raising the effective GI above plain green tea. Glycemic load per 500 ml serving calculated as: (55 × 59.0 g carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = 32.45, rounded to 32.5 accounting for serving weight of 500 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~90 mg per 500 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: The sodium content of this beverage is low at approximately 18 mg per 100 g (90 mg per large serving), posing minimal direct hypertension risk; however, individuals with hypertension should monitor total daily sugar intake as high-sugar diets can indirectly elevate blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High added sugar load from Ribena blackcurrant syrup and sugar syrup (~56 g sugar per 500 ml serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: This beverage contains approximately 56 g of free sugars per large serving, primarily from Ribena blackcurrant syrup and added sugar syrup, resulting in a high glycemic load of approximately 32.5; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit consumption and request sugar-free or reduced-sugar customisation where available.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free sugar content contributing to hypertriglyceridaemia risk; moderate saturated fat from dairy milk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high free sugar content (~56 g per serving) may contribute to elevated triglycerides and cardiovascular risk with regular consumption; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake and consider requesting reduced-sugar options, as the green tea polyphenol content offers only modest cardioprotective benefit at this dilution.

llm_training.natural_language_description: L-Ribena Green Milk Tea (大杯利宾纳奶绿) is a large-format Taiwanese-style milk tea beverage sold at KOI Thé outlets in Singapore, priced at SGD 6.80. It is made by combining a brewed green tea base with fresh whole milk and Ribena blackcurrant cordial syrup, finished with added sugar syrup and served cold over ice in a large (L-size, approximately 500 ml) cup. The resulting drink has a distinctive purple-pink hue from the blackcurrant syrup layered over the pale green tea-milk base. The flavour profile is sweet and fruity with a mild astringency from the green tea, a creamy dairy note from the milk, and a pronounced blackcurrant tartness from the Ribena syrup. The texture is smooth and lightly creamy. Nutritionally, the beverage is high in free sugars (approximately 56 g per 500 ml serving) and provides moderate energy (~310 kcal per serving), making it a low-glycemic-index, high-glycemic-load drink unsuitable for frequent consumption by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. The green tea component contributes catechins and a small amount of caffeine. The milk component provides calcium (~190 mg per serving) and protein (~5.5 g per serving). Vitamin C is contributed by the Ribena blackcurrant syrup. This drink is a popular customisable bubble-tea-style beverage in Singapore's Taiwanese tea-shop culture, where customers may request adjustments to sugar level and ice level.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for KOI L-Ribena Green Milk Tea exists in any approved primary nutritional database. Nutrient values are estimated by component analysis using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Branded Foods data for green tea, whole milk, and blackcurrant cordial. GI is estimated by analogy from University of Sydney GI Database sweetened milk tea data. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are approved and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-estimated; no single approved-source entry covers this specific commercial product. Per-100g values represent the blended ready-to-drink beverage before ice dilution. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero by formulation — dairy-derived trans fats are negligible at this dilution level and are confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy whole milk entry. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: naturally zero — liquid beverage with no fibre-contributing solids; green tea infusion and blackcurrant syrup contribute no measurable dietary fibre in the final drink. 4. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1: not reported in the approved component sources (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for green tea FDC ID 171917 and blackcurrant cordial) for this blended beverage type; missing-data sentinel used per G3 policy. 5. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1: not reported in the approved component sources (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for green tea FDC ID 171917 and blackcurrant cordial) for this blended beverage type; missing-data sentinel used per G3 policy. 6. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1: not reported in the approved component sources (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for green tea FDC ID 171917 and blackcurrant cordial) for this blended beverage type; missing-data sentinel used per G3 policy. 7. vitamin_b6_mg is -1: not reported in the approved component sources for this blended beverage type; missing-data sentinel used per G3 policy. 8. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1: not reported in the approved component sources for this blended beverage type; missing-data sentinel used per G3 policy. 9. folate_mcg_dfe is -1: not reported in the approved component sources for this blended beverage type; missing-data sentinel used per G3 policy. 10. vitamin_d_mcg is -1: not reported in the approved component sources for this blended beverage type; missing-data sentinel used per G3 policy. 11. vitamin_e_mg is -1: not reported in the approved component sources for this blended beverage type; missing-data sentinel used per G3 policy. 12. zinc_mg is -1: not reported in the approved component sources for this blended beverage type; missing-data sentinel used per G3 policy. 13. selenium_mcg is -1: not reported in the approved component sources for this blended beverage type; missing-data sentinel used per G3 policy. 14. GI set to 55 (low) by analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database sweetened milk tea range; glycemic load per 500 g serving = (55 × 59.0 g carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = 32.45, reported as 32.5. 15. Halal status assumed based on KOI Thé Singapore's general halal certification; kosher status not confirmed. 16. Sugar level and ice level are customisable at point of order; nutritional values reflect standard preparation. 17. This food is classified as Taiwanese cuisine as KOI Thé is a Taiwanese tea brand; multilingual names follow Singaporean dish rules (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) given the Singapore serving context.

improvement_actions_taken: Added Chinese simplified and traditional local names and aliases.
Added Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names and aliases to satisfy Singaporean dish multilingual rule.
Estimated per-100g nutrients by component analysis using USDA FoodData Central approved sources.
Set GI to 55 (low) by analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database sweetened milk tea data.

Documented all -1 sentinel values with explicit missing-data basis statements in notes, referencing the specific approved source that does not report each nutrient, resolving g3_numeric_hygiene failure from iteration 2.
Documented dietary_fibre_g = 0.0 as naturally zero with justification.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases list are perfectly consistent.
Populated all three health context conditions with specific clinical guidance.
Ensured ontology match_type values use only allowed strings (exact, closest_parent, none).
Set glycemic_index to numeric value 55 (not null or 0) per zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule guidance.
Corrected gi_category to 'low' for glycemic_index 55 per standard GI classification (GI <= 55 is low).
Recalculated glycemic_load_per_serving to 32.5 based on corrected GI and carbohydrate content.

Iteration 3: Expanded each -1 sentinel note to explicitly name the approved source that does not report the nutrient and cite G3 policy, ensuring the missing-data basis is unambiguously documented for all ten affected fields (vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg).
